creator: core.dumped.sm@gmail.com
text:
I am using Asus USB-N11 wireless card as host AP:
ugen4.2: <802.11 n WLAN Ralink> at usbus4, cfg=0 md=HOST spd=HIGH (480Mbps) pwr=ON

  bLength = 0x0012 
  bDescriptorType = 0x0001 
  bcdUSB = 0x0200 
  bDeviceClass = 0x0000 
  bDeviceSubClass = 0x0000 
  bDeviceProtocol = 0x0000 
  bMaxPacketSize0 = 0x0040 
  idVendor = 0x1761 
  idProduct = 0x0b05 
  bcdDevice = 0x0101 
  iManufacturer = 0x0001  <Ralink>
  iProduct = 0x0002  <802.11 n WLAN>
  iSerialNumber = 0x0003  <1.0>
  bNumConfigurations = 0x0001 
Regularly there are unpredictable reboot because of problems with the device driver:

last trap:

How-To-Repeat: Plug Asus USB-N11 device.
Configure the system to use the device as a host AP.
Wait indefinitely: sometimes a kernel panic occurs without any connection to the host AP.
